comment.body:
"If volatility catches up here during the AM session, and call skew bids up by 91.9% at least, the spot can break up to the upper part of the centroid range, but the upper wing of 6480 (margin of error of 6485) is kinda unbreakable after 1.30PM. It can be broken before only, if spot can holds above the upside pivot and realized volatility suppresses while call skew remains up. This is the right-tail scenario.
In this case 6500/6505 is the limit, there vomma will mange the spot and reject it back."

It can be broken before only, if spot can holds above the upside pivot and realized volatility suppresses while call skew remains up. This is the right-tail scenario.
In this case 6500/6505 is the limit, there vomma will mange the spot and reject it back."

comment.body:
This week presents a dual test for the U.S. labor market. Friday's monthly report will finalize the decision for September, while Tuesday's annual revision will paint a truer picture of the economy's underlying health over the past year.

The narrative is clearly shifting from the certainty of a September cut to the uncertainty of the Fed's path forward. While the massive data revision is startling, it is largely anticipated and may simply provide justification for a slower, more cautious easing cycle. Going forward, the unemployment rate and inflation data will become even more critical in determining whether the Federal Reserve embarks on a series of consecutive rate cuts.
comment.raw_body:
